Player.title: Player
Player.description: Represents a player account in the Axiom Frontier game, including their core profile, currency balance, and associated entities.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Player entity.

username.description: The player's chosen username, unique across the game.

email.description: The player's email address, used for communication and account recovery. (Not used for direct authentication/password storage)

creationDate.description: The date and time when the player account was created.

lastLoginDate.description: The date and time of the player's last successful login.

balance.description: The amount of in-game currency the player possesses.

agentIds.description: References to Agents owned by this player. (Relationship: Player 1:N Agent)

playerInventoryItemIds.description: References to PlayerInventoryItem entities representing the items currently owned by this player. (Relationship: Player 1:N PlayerInventoryItem)

playerQuestStatusIds.description: References to PlayerQuestStatus entities tracking this player's progress on various quests. (Relationship: Player 1:N PlayerQuestStatus)

playerStatus.description: The current status of the player account (e.g., 'active', 'suspended', 'banned').

Agent.title: Agent
Agent.description: Represents an in-game agent, controlled by a player, capable of performing various actions like gathering, combat, and trading.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Agent entity.

playerId.description: Reference to the Player who owns this agent. (Relationship: Player 1:N Agent)

agentType.description: The primary role or specialization of the agent (e.g., 'gatherer', 'combatant', 'trader', 'explorer').

level.description: The current experience level of the agent.

experience.description: The current experience points accumulated by the agent towards the next level.

currentLocationX.description: The X coordinate of the agent's current position in the game world.

currentLocationY.description: The Y coordinate of the agent's current position in the game world.

currentLocationZ.description: The Z coordinate of the agent's current position in the game world.

status.description: The current activity status of the agent (e.g., 'idle', 'gathering', 'fighting', 'traveling').

health.description: The current health points of the agent.

energy.description: The current energy points of the agent, used for actions.

equippedPlayerInventoryItemIds.description: References to PlayerInventoryItem entities that are currently equipped by this agent. (Relationship: Agent N:N PlayerInventoryItem)

GameItem.title: GameItem
GameItem.description: Defines a generic item type that can exist in the game world, be looted, or be part of a player's inventory.

id.description: Unique identifier for the GameItem entity.

name.description: The display name of the item.

description.description: A detailed description of the item and its properties.

rarity.description: The rarity level of the item (e.g., 'common', 'uncommon', 'rare', 'epic', 'legendary').

value.description: The base value of the item in in-game currency for trading or selling.

weight.description: The weight of the item, affecting inventory capacity.

gameResourceId.description: Reference to the GameResource entity that provides the visual model or texture for this item. (Relationship: GameItem 1:1 GameResource)

PlayerInventoryItem.title: PlayerInventoryItem
PlayerInventoryItem.description: Represents a specific instance of a GameItem owned by a player, allowing tracking of quantity and individual item properties.

id.description: Unique identifier for this specific PlayerInventoryItem instance.

playerId.description: Reference to the Player who owns this item instance. (Relationship: Player 1:N PlayerInventoryItem)

gameItemId.description: Reference to the GameItem definition for this item instance. (Relationship: GameItem 1:N PlayerInventoryItem)

quantity.description: The number of units of this item type owned in this inventory slot. Relevant for stackable items.

durability.description: The current durability or condition of the item, if applicable.

acquisitionDate.description: The date and time when this item instance was acquired by the player.

StoreProduct.title: StoreProduct
StoreProduct.description: Represents an item or offering available for purchase in the Axiom Storefront using real-world currency.

id.description: Unique identifier for the StoreProduct entity.

name.description: The display name of the product in the storefront.

description.description: A description of the product and what it offers.

price.description: The price of the product in real-world currency.

currencyType.description: The real-world currency in which the price is denominated (e.g., 'USD', 'EUR').

gameItemId.description: Optional: Reference to the GameItem entity that this product grants upon purchase. (Relationship: StoreProduct 1:1 GameItem)

imageUrl.description: The URL of the image displaying the product in the storefront.

isActive.description: Indicates whether the product is currently available for purchase in the storefront.

Transaction.title: Transaction
Transaction.description: Records a purchase transaction made by a player in the Axiom Storefront.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Transaction entity.

playerId.description: Reference to the Player who made the purchase. (Relationship: Player 1:N Transaction)

storeProductId.description: Reference to the StoreProduct that was purchased. (Relationship: StoreProduct 1:N Transaction)

transactionDate.description: The date and time when the transaction occurred.

amount.description: The amount of real-world currency paid for the product.

currencyType.description: The real-world currency type used for the transaction (e.g., 'USD', 'EUR').

paymentStatus.description: The current status of the payment (e.g., 'pending', 'completed', 'failed', 'refunded').

paymentProviderTransactionId.description: The transaction ID provided by the payment gateway (e.g., PayPal).

WorldState.title: WorldState
WorldState.description: Represents the global, persistent state of the game world, including critical metrics and active elements.

id.description: Unique identifier for the WorldState entity. (Intended as a singleton, e.g., 'world-1')

civilizationIndex.description: A calculated index reflecting the overall advancement and health of the in-game civilization(s).

economyInflationRate.description: The current inflation rate affecting the in-game economy.

lastHeartbeatTime.description: The timestamp of the last successful 'world heartbeat' cron job execution.

activeQuestIds.description: References to Quest entities that are currently active and available to players in the world. (Relationship: WorldState N:N Quest)

Quest.title: Quest
Quest.description: Defines a quest within the game, which can be dynamically generated by AI or pre-defined.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Quest entity.

description.description: A detailed description of the quest's objectives and lore.

questGiverNpcId.description: Optional: Reference to the NPC entity who gives this quest. (Relationship: NPC 1:N Quest)

rewardItemIds.description: Optional: References to GameItem entities rewarded upon quest completion. (Relationship: Quest N:N GameItem)

rewardCurrency.description: Optional: The amount of in-game currency rewarded upon quest completion.

difficulty.description: The estimated difficulty level of the quest (e.g., 'easy', 'medium', 'hard').

status.description: The global status of the quest (e.g., 'available', 'retired').

generatedByAI.description: Indicates whether this quest was dynamically generated by the AI.

generationPrompt.description: Optional: The prompt used by the AI to generate this quest, for auditing and debugging.

youtubeScriptId.description: Optional: Reference to a YouTubeScript entity if a script related to this quest was generated. (Relationship: Quest 1:1 YouTubeScript)

PlayerQuestStatus.title: PlayerQuestStatus
PlayerQuestStatus.description: Tracks a specific player's progress and status on a particular quest.

id.description: Unique identifier for the PlayerQuestStatus entity.

playerId.description: Reference to the Player undertaking this quest. (Relationship: Player 1:N PlayerQuestStatus)

questId.description: Reference to the Quest being tracked. (Relationship: Quest 1:N PlayerQuestStatus)

status.description: The player's current status for this quest (e.g., 'not_started', 'in_progress', 'completed', 'failed').

currentProgress.description: A numerical representation of the player's progress on the quest (e.g., percentage, number of items collected).

lastUpdateDate.description: The date and time when the quest status was last updated.

startDate.description: Optional: The date and time when the player started this quest.

completionDate.description: Optional: The date and time when the player completed (or failed) this quest.

NPC.title: NPC
NPC.description: Represents a Non-Player Character in the game world, potentially with AI-generated personalities.

id.description: Unique identifier for the NPC entity.

name.description: The display name of the NPC.

personalityDescription.description: A description of the NPC's personality, potentially generated by AI.

npcType.description: The role or type of the NPC (e.g., 'trader', 'quest_giver', 'enemy', 'villager').

locationTag.description: A tag indicating the NPC's general location or habitat (e.g., 'village_center', 'forest_edge', 'space_station_dock').

gameResourceId.description: Reference to the GameResource entity that provides the visual model for this NPC. (Relationship: NPC 1:1 GameResource)

generatedByAI.description: Indicates whether this NPC's personality or other attributes were dynamically generated by the AI.

generationPrompt.description: Optional: The prompt used by the AI to generate this NPC's characteristics, for auditing and debugging.

GameResource.title: GameResource
GameResource.description: Represents a generic in-game asset (e.g., 3D model, texture, sound effect) managed by Firebase Storage.

id.description: Unique identifier for the GameResource entity.

name.description: The display name of the resource.

description.description: A description of what the resource represents (e.g., '3D model for basic sword', 'texture for space suit').

storagePath.description: The path or URI to the asset file within Firebase Storage.

version.description: The version identifier for this specific resource, allowing for updates.

YouTubeScript.title: YouTubeScript
YouTubeScript.description: Represents a script for a YouTube video, dynamically generated by the AI for content creation.

id.description: Unique identifier for the YouTubeScript entity.

title.description: The title of the generated YouTube script.

scriptContent.description: The full textual content of the YouTube script.

generatedDate.description: The date and time when the script was generated.

relatedQuestId.description: Optional: Reference to a Quest entity if this script is related to a specific in-game quest. (Relationship: YouTubeScript 1:1 Quest)

generationPrompt.description: Optional: The prompt used by the AI to generate this YouTube script, for auditing and debugging.

AdminUser.title: AdminUser
AdminUser.description: Represents an administrator user with access to the Axiom Frontier Admin Console.

id.description: Unique identifier for the AdminUser entity. (Typically corresponds to Firebase Authentication UID)

email.description: The email address of the administrator. (Not used for direct authentication/password storage)

roles.description: A list of roles assigned to the administrator, defining their permissions (e.g., 'super_admin', 'game_manager', 'content_editor').

lastLoginDate.description: The date and time of the administrator's last successful login.

GameParameter.title: GameParameter
GameParameter.description: Represents a configurable game world parameter that can be adjusted via the Admin Console.

name.description: The human-readable name of the game parameter.

description.description: A detailed description of what this parameter controls.

valueString.description: Optional: The value of the parameter if it is a string type.

valueNumber.description: Optional: The value of the parameter if it is a numeric type.

valueBoolean.description: Optional: The value of the parameter if it is a boolean type.

lastUpdatedByAdminId.description: Reference to the AdminUser who last modified this parameter. (Relationship: AdminUser 1:N GameParameter)

lastUpdateTime.description: The date and time when this parameter was last updated.

definition.description: Top-level collection for individual player profiles. Document ID MUST match Firebase Authentication UID. Access is restricted to the player themselves (owner-only read/write). Includes denormalized 'id' for direct authorization comparison.

params.name: playerId
params.description: The unique Firebase Authentication UID of the player, serving as the document ID.

definition.description: Subcollection for agents owned by a specific player. Access is restricted to the parent player. Authorization is implicitly handled by the path structure (request.auth.uid == playerId).

params.name: playerId
params.description: The unique Firebase Authentication UID of the owning player.

params.name: agentId
params.description: The unique identifier for the agent.

definition.description: Top-level collection for generic game item definitions. Read-only for all players, write access for administrators.

params.name: gameItemId
params.description: The unique identifier for the game item definition.

definition.description: Subcollection for specific instances of items owned by a player. Access is restricted to the parent player. Authorization is implicitly handled by the path structure (request.auth.uid == playerId).

params.name: playerId
params.description: The unique Firebase Authentication UID of the owning player.

params.name: playerInventoryItemId
params.description: The unique identifier for this specific player inventory item instance.

definition.description: Top-level collection for products available in the game's storefront. Read-only for all players, write access for administrators.

params.name: storeProductId
params.description: The unique identifier for the store product.

definition.description: Subcollection for recording purchase transactions made by a specific player. Read-only for the parent player, write access only via Cloud Functions (system) or administrators. Authorization is implicitly handled by the path structure (request.auth.uid == playerId).

params.name: playerId
params.description: The unique Firebase Authentication UID of the player who made the transaction.

params.name: transactionId
params.description: The unique identifier for the transaction record.

definition.description: Singleton document representing the global, persistent state of the game world. Read-only for all players, write access only via scheduled Cloud Functions (world heartbeat) or administrators. 'singleton' is a fixed document ID.
Top-level collection for global quest definitions. Read-only for all players (if 'status' is 'available'), write access for administrators or system (AI generation).

params.name: questId
params.description: The unique identifier for the quest definition.

definition.description: Subcollection tracking a specific player's progress and status on various quests. Access is restricted to the parent player. Authorization is implicitly handled by the path structure (request.auth.uid == playerId).

params.name: playerId
params.description: The unique Firebase Authentication UID of the player tracking this quest.

params.name: playerQuestStatusId
params.description: The unique identifier for the player's quest status instance.

definition.description: Top-level collection for Non-Player Character definitions. Read-only for all players, write access for administrators or system (AI generation).

params.name: npcId
params.description: The unique identifier for the NPC definition.

definition.description: Top-level collection for definitions of game assets (models, textures, sounds). Read-only for all players, write access for administrators.

params.name: gameResourceId
params.description: The unique identifier for the game resource definition.

definition.description: Top-level collection for AI-generated YouTube video scripts. Read/write access restricted to administrators or specific content editor roles.

params.name: youtubeScriptId
params.description: The unique identifier for the YouTube script.

definition.description: Top-level collection for administrative user roles. Document ID MUST match Firebase Authentication UID. Read access for any authenticated user to check their own roles. Write access restricted to super-administrators. This collection serves as the source for Database-Backed Access Control (DBAC), enabling 'get()' calls in rules to determine administrative permissions without custom claims.

params.name: adminUserId
params.description: The unique Firebase Authentication UID of the administrator, serving as the document ID.

definition.description: Top-level collection for configurable game world parameters. Read-only for all players, write access for administrators. The 'lastUpdatedByAdminId' field is for audit, not direct authorization.

params.name: gameParameterId
params.description: The unique identifier for the game parameter (e.g., 'loot_drop_rate').

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore data structure for Axiom Frontier is designed around a core principle of Authorization Independence, primarily achieved through structural segregation and path-based ownership. This ensures security rules are simple, robust, and easily debuggable, minimizing reliance on `get()` calls for authorization context and enabling secure list operations (QAPs).

**Authorization Independence (Denormalization Strategy):**
1.  **Player-Owned Data:** All entities directly owned by a player (Agents, PlayerInventoryItems, Transactions, PlayerQuestStatuses) are stored in subcollections under the `/players/{playerId}` path. This establishes a clear hierarchical ownership model where `request.auth.uid` directly maps to `playerId` in the path (e.g., `/players/$(request.auth.uid)/agents/{agentId}`). This eliminates the need for `get()` calls to a parent document to verify ownership, making rules atomic and performant.
2.  **Global Authorization Context (DBAC):** For administrative roles, a dedicated `/admin_users/{adminUserId}` collection is used. An `isAdmin()` or `isSuperAdmin()` function within security rules would perform a single `get()` operation on this collection using `request.auth.uid` to check the user's roles. This `get()` is specifically for retrieving the user's *authorization context*, not for *data dependency* between application entities. This aligns with the principle by centralizing role management and avoiding distributed authorization checks across related data documents.

**QAPs (Rules are not Filters):**
1.  **Structural Segregation:** Data with different security postures is never mixed in the same collection. For example, player-specific inventory is in `/players/{playerId}/inventory_items`, distinct from global item definitions in `/game_items`. Quests (`/quests`) are global definitions, while player progress is in `/players/{playerId}/quest_statuses`.
2.  **Public Read Collections:** Global entities like `GameItem`, `StoreProduct`, `NPC`, `GameResource`, and `GameParameter` are in top-level collections and are generally read-only for all users (`true`). This inherently supports secure `list` operations as all listed data is publicly accessible.
3.  **Filtered Public Data:** The `/quests` collection stores global quest definitions. While accessible to all, rules would enforce that only quests with `status == 'available'` are readable by regular players, ensuring that listing only returns relevant, public quests. The `WorldState` singleton document is also publicly readable.

**Other Principles Applied:**
*   **DBAC:** Roles for administrators are stored in the `/admin_users` collection, with authorization rules relying solely on `request.auth.uid` to query this collection for roles, rather than custom claims.
*   **Explicit State Modeling:** Entities like `Player`, `Agent`, `Quest`, `PlayerQuestStatus`, `StoreProduct`, and `Transaction` utilize a single `status` field to denote their current state, making application logic and security rules clear.
*   **Radical Consistency:** Wildcards are descriptive (e.g., `{playerId}`, `{agentId}`). Fields like `playerId` are consistently used for relationships, and the `id` field of documents matches the document ID for player-owned entities.
